sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.3 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Let$$and$$be faithful non-linear irreducible characters of a finite group$G$. There are non-solvable groups$G$giving examples when the product$$is again an irreducible character (for some of such$,$). One example is$G=SL_2(5)$with two irreducible characters of degree 2. In (I.\,Zisser, Israel J.~Math., 84, no.\,1--2 (1993), 147--151) it is proved that such an example exists in an alternating group...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
